headline: Model Context Protocol broke out as the connective layer for autonomous multi-tool agents this month.
body: Model Context Protocol and multi-tool integration led the window's rotation and both registered as breakouts, framed as the way LLMs discover and chain tools without hardcoded APIs and orchestrate enterprise data across databases, BI, and vector stores. Agent latency work moved alongside it, with an 80% execution speedup on the Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform via parallel execution, OpenTelemetry instrumentation, and profiling. On the model side, Sonnet 5 landed to mixed reviews on pricing and coding performance, while OpenAI signaled a merger of chatbots and agents and raised safety concerns around self-sustaining research loops. The White House imposed then dropped a two-week restriction on Anthropic models.
